title: DeepPhenoTree - CVPPA Data Challenge
terms:
### Registration

* All participants must create a single Codabench account with a valid e-mail address.  
* Teams must register under one shared account and provide a team-wide contact e-mail that is reachable by every member.  
* Creating multiple accounts to bypass submission limits is prohibited and will lead to immediate disqualification.

### Submission Rules

* All submissions have to be uploaded through the Codabench platform.
* Development phase - maximum 5 submissions per day and 100 submissions in total.
* Blind-evaluation phase - maximum 3 submissions per day and 10 submissions in total.
* Submissions must respect the format described on the Data page (YOLO-style .txt files, see example).
* Predictions must be generated automatically; manual tweaking of the ground-truth is not allowed.
* Only data that are publicly available or that participants are explicitly authorized to use may be employed.

### Data Usage

* The challenge uses the DeepPhenoTree - Apple Edition dataset, which is publicly available at: https://entrepot.recherche.data.gouv.fr/dataset.xhtml?persistentId=doi:10.57745/NORPF1 .
* The dataset is released under the etalab 2.0 license (see the dataset page).
* By participating you agree to respect the license terms and to cite the dataset as indicated on the repository.

### Reproducibility & Code Submission

* Participants who wish to be considered for prizes or for inclusion in the CVPPA workshop proceedings must upload their final prediction code between 26 June and 5  July 2026.
* The code must enable the organizers to reproduce the blind-phase predictions on the same infrastructure.
* Submissions that cannot be reproduced will be excluded from prize consideration.

### Publication

* By taking part you grant the organizers permission to include aggregated, anonymized results in academic papers and workshop proceedings.
* Teams achieving notable performance encourage to submit and present a  paper at the CVPPA workshop (8-9 September  2026), subject to normal peer-review.
* Participants retain ownership of their source code and any accompanying manuscript.

### Disqualification

* The organizers reserve the right to disqualify any submission that:
  	 * Violates any of the rules above,
  	 * Attempts to exploit the evaluation infrastructure, or
  	 * Undermines the fairness of the competition.
 * Disqualification decisions are final.

### Contact

For any questions, clarifications or support, please contact the challenge organizers at:  

> challenge2026-deepphenotree@inrae.fr

pages.title: DeepPhenoTree – ECCV 2026 CVPPA Data Challenge
pages.content: # DeepPhenoTree – ECCV 2026 CVPPA Data Challenge The objective of this challenge is to develop models capable of detecting objects representative of apple tree phenological stages. ## Why a Phenology Detection Challenge? Phenology is a key indicator of plant development, from dormancy to harvest, and plays a central role in orchard management. However, it is still largely assessed manually, making it time-consuming and difficult to scale. This challenge introduces a standardized benchmark for phenological object detection under real-world conditions. Collected across four apple European experimental orchard sites [1], the dataset captures strong variability across multiple sites, acquisition conditions, and phenological stages, while benefiting from controlled illumination. The task is particularly challenging due to: - Variability across sites and acquisition conditions, - Different object sizes and occlusions within tree canopies, - High intra-class similarity between adjacent phenological stages All images were acquired using a standardized imaging platform under controlled illumination conditions, ensuring consistent visual quality across sites while preserving real open field variability conditions. A key objective of this challenge is to publicly promote models that generalize across sites and conditions. ## Challenge Overview Given an input image of apple trees, participants are asked to design a model that predict bounding boxes for objects associated with Biologische Bundesanstalt,Bundessortenamt et CHemische Industrie (or BBCH) [2] including in three main phenological stages: from Dormant to Flowering (BBCH 00-69), Fruitlet (BBCH 71-77) and Fruit (BBCH 81-89). Ground truth annotations consist of manually labeled bounding boxes with corresponding main phenological stages. Ground-truth boxes are provided in YOLO format (class 0 = flowering, 1 = fruitlet, 2 = fruit). Models are evaluated automatically upon each submission. During the Development Phase, participants receive real-time evaluation scores on a public test set and can iteratively improve their models. During the Blind Evaluation Phase, predictions are generated on a separate private test set, and final scores are only revealed at the end of the competition to ensure fair ranking. ## Contribution ### Paper (under review) Metuarea Herearii, Ousseini Hamza Abdoul-Djalil, Guerra Walter, Zuffa Francesca, Panzeri Francesco, Patocchi Andrea, Lozano Lidia, Van Hoye Shauny, Laurens François, Labrosse Jeremy, Rasti Pejman & Rousseau David. (2026, February 27). DeepPhenoTree – Apple Edition: A multi-site apple phenology RGB annotated dataset with deep learning baseline models (Version 1) [Preprint]. Research Square. https://doi.org/10.21203/rs.3.rs-8977752/v1 ### Resources The [DeepPhenoTree](https://napari-hub.org/plugins/deepphenotree) plugin is an user interface of our paper DeepPhenoTree – Apple Edition (paper above), and is designed to support experimentation and reproducibility. The plugin allows users to: - Run baseline inference of our RT-DETR models (Flowering, Fruitlet, Fruit), - Visualize predictions interactively, - Export results in YOLO format. This tool is optional and provided for convenience; participants are free to use their own pipelines. ## Acknowledgments The DeepPhenoTree Data Challenge is led by **IRHS**, **Université d'Angers**, **INRAe**, **Institut Agro** and four research institutes of the apple REFPOP consortium : **IRTA** (Spain), **Research Centre Laimburg** (Italy), **Agroscope** (Switzerland) and **Better3fruit N.V.** (Belgium). It is supported by : - The **European Union’s Horizon Europe research innovation programme** under grant agreement No 101094587 (PHENET) - The French National Research Agency (ANR) as part of the **Programme d'Investissements d'Avenir** under grant agreement ANR-11-INBS-0012 (PHENOME-EMPHASIS) - The French National Research Agency (ANR) as part of the **France 2030** investment plan under grant agreement ANR-22-PEAE-0012 (AGROECOPHEN) ## Reference [1] Jung, M., Roth, M., Aranzana, M. J., Auwerkerken, A., Bink, M., Denancé, C., Dujak, C., Durel, C.-E., Font i Forcada, C., Cantin, C. M., Guerra, W., Howard, N. P., Keller, B., Lewandowski, M., Ordidge, M., Rymenants, M., Sanin, N., Studer, B., Zurawicz, E., Laurens, F., Patocchi, A., & Muranty, H. (2020). The apple REFPOP—a reference population for genomics-assisted breeding in apple. Horticulture Research, 7, Article 66. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41438-020-0298-7 [2] Meier, U., Graf, H., Hack, H., Hess, M., Kennel, W., Klose, R., Mappes, D., Seipp, D., Stauss, R., Streif, J., & Boom, T. V. D. (1994). Phenological growth stages of pome fruits (Malus domestica Borkh. and Pyrus communis L.), stone fruits (Prunus species), currants (Ribes species) and strawberry (Fragaria × ananassa Duch.). --- ## Contact For questions, clarifications, or to request additional resources, please contact the organizers at: **challenge2026-deepphenotree@inrae.fr** We look forward to seeing your innovative approaches to phenological detection!

pages.title: Challenge and Timeline
pages.content:
# Challenge and Timeline
## Task

Given an input image of apple trees, a participating system must output, for each instance in the image, a bounding box and the corresponding phenological stage label.

* **Input:** An RGB image of apple trees (provided in the `images/` folder).
* **Output:**  A `.txt` file with the same base name as the image, containing one detection per line:
```txt
<class_id> <x_center> <y_center> <width> <height> <confidence>
```
where :
- **class_id** (0 = flowering, 1 = fruitlet, 2 = fruit)
- **x_center, y_center, width, height** (bounding boxes coordinates normalized to image width/height in YOLO-style format, one line per detection)
- **confidence** is the confidence the model outputs for the given bounding box (if not provided, it default to 1)

The dataset contains images with multiple instances per image, corresponding to different parts of the tree at various phenological stages. Systems must produce predictions for all detectable instances in each image.

Each object should be detected only once (no duplicate detections with high overlap). See **Competition metric** section for more details.

## Submission Format

Follow the submission guidelines described below.

### 1. Model development

Download the data for the Development stage. Feel free to use your split train/val structure, external data or pretrained models.
A baseline implementation and additional resources may be provided to facilitate reproducibility and benchmarking.

### 2. Prediction Generation

For each image in test set, participants must generate a prediction file in YOLO format using python file. The python file must define a class named Model. This class must be loadable by the evaluation system and expose a prediction method with the following structure :

```
class Model:
    def __init__(self, ...):
        ...

    def predict(self, image_path: str):
        ...
```

Predict :
- Input : Path to a test image
- Output : A list of strings in YOLO format as follow:

```
class_id x_center y_center width height confidence
```
where `class_id` is class index and  `x_center y_center width height` are coordinates of bounding box (in range [0,1]) normalized by image width height, `confidence` is the confidence level (in range [0,1]).

### 3. Package your submission

Create a zip archive named `deepphenotree_challenge2026.zip` with the following structure:

```
deepphenotree_challenge2026/
├─ model.py
├─ weights.pth (or other format)
├─ params.yml (if needed by your model)
└─ README.md   (optional short description)
```

Participants may rely on standard Python deep learning libraries. Any non-standard dependency required for inference must be clearly specified in the README file.

Upload the zip on the **Codabench** competition page.

* Provide predictions for **all images** in the test set.
* All coordinates must be floating-point numbers between 0 and 1.
* Files must be UTF-8 encoded with Unix line endings.

## Competition Metric

Submissions are scored automatically with **Mean Average Precision (mAP)** at IoU 0.5, the standard metric for object-detection challenges.

* **mAP@0.5:0.95** -  Primary leaderboard score (average over IoU thresholds 0.5-0.95).
* **mAP@0.5** - Secondary score.

The evaluation pipeline:

1. For each image, predictions are matched to ground-truth boxes using the Hungarian algorithm.
2. True positives are counted when IoU ≥ 0.5 and class matches.
3. Precision-recall curves are computed per class, then averaged to obtain mAP.

## Timeline

| Date (2026) | Milestone |
|-------------|-----------|
| **15 April** | Codabench competition page goes live; leaderboard starts updating;|
| **15 April** | Training set released|
| **26 June** | Development Phase closes; no more leaderboard updates. |
| **26 June - 5 July** | Deadline for uploading final prediction code / Docker image. |
| **5-6  July** | Final leaderboard published; winners announced. |
| **10  July** | Paper submission deadline (EasyChair). |
| **14 August** | Acceptance notifications. |
| **September 2026** | Presentation at **CVPPA Workshop @ ECCV 2026 (Malmö, Sweden)**. |

All deadlines are **23:59 AoE** (Anywhere on Earth) unless otherwise noted

pages.title: Dataset and Baseline
pages.content:
# Dataset and Baseline

## Dataset

The challenge is based on the **DeepPhenoTree – Apple Edition**

| Component | Description | Link |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| **DeepPhenoTree – Apple Edition** | 808 annotated RGB images (Switzerland, Belgium, Spain, Italy) collected in 2025 covering flowering, fruitlet, and fruit. | https://entrepot.recherche.data.gouv.fr/dataset.xhtml?persistentId=doi:10.57745/NORPF1 [2] |
| **License** | etalab 2.0 (open reuse with citation). | – |
| **Metadata** | Includes site and BBCH main stage distribution. | – |

The dataset is split by the organizers into a public dataset that you can split for train/val and a private development test set (accessed via Codabench during the development phase), and a blind test set used for the final evaluation of the model.

| Subset                               | Description                                                                   | Size     |
| ------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- |
| **Train / Validation (public)**      | Labeled dataset provided to participants for model development                | 472 / 86 |
| **Development Test (private)** | Hidden test set during the development phase via Codabench | 86       |
| **Blind Test (private)**             | Hidden test set used for final evaluation                                     | 164      |

Participants are free to split the public dataset into training and validation subsets as needed. The development test set allows participants to benchmark their models. Final rankings will be based solely on performance on the blind test set.


## Baseline Results

For the purpose of this challenge, we expect models for three stages.

In our paper, baseline performance is reported using a cross-validation protocol. We trained three separate baseline models, one for each task (Flowering, Fruitlet, Fruit). These models are specialized and optimized independently. Models are trained and evaluated across five folds, and the best-performing model is selected based on test performance. For the purpose of this challenge, we report results obtained from the best fold, corresponding to the model achieving the highest test score (corresponding to blind phase for this data challenge).

| Model | mAP@0.5 | mAP@0.5:0.95 |
|-------|---------|--------------|
| **Fruit-only fine-tuned** | 0.86 | 0.58 |
| **Fruitlet-only fine-tuned** | 0.84 | 0.54 |
| **Flowering-only fine-tuned** | 0.68 | 0.39 |

**For this challenge, participants are encouraged to go beyond this setting and propose a unified or multi-task model capable of jointly addressing all three phenological stages.**

Below, we show results for all stages based on our trained model on Fruit-only fine-tuned to indicate a baseline for participant. Note that the baseline does not model cross-stage relationships, which opens opportunities for improved performance through multi-task or unified approaches.

| Model | mAP@0.5 | mAP@0.5:0.95 |
|-------|---------|--------------|
| **Baseline** | 0.29 | 0.16 |

pages.title: FAQ
pages.content:
# FAQ

❓ Can I use external data or pretrained models?

Yes. Participants are allowed to use external datasets or pretrained models (e.g., ImageNet, COCO, or self-supervised models), unless explicitly stated otherwise. All training procedures must remain reproducible, and participants are expected to clearly document any external resources used in their README.md.

❓ Do I need to use the provided train/validation split?

No. The provided split is recommended but not mandatory. Participants are free to define their own train/validation split.

❓ What environment is used for the ingestion/scoring of my model?

The evaluation is performed on a dedicated runner equipped with an NVIDIA V100 (32GB) GPU.

Time Limit: The entire inference process (loading the model and processing all test images) must be completed within 30 minutes. Submissions exceeding this limit will be automatically timed out.

Environment: All scripts are executed within a controlled Docker container to ensure reproducibility. You can access the official base image here: `registry.forge.inrae.fr/imhorphen/deepphenotree/cvppa-2026-base:v1`.

Software Stack: To inspect the pre-installed libraries and their exact versions, you can run the following command:
```bash
podman run --rm \
    registry.forge.inrae.fr/imhorphen/deepphenotree/cvppa-2026-base:v1 \
    pip freeze
```

❓ What happens if my submission does not follow the required format?

Submissions are evaluated automatically. Any submission that does not follow the required structure or interface may fail during ingestion and will not be evaluated. Participants are strongly encouraged to validate their submission before uploading.

❓ Can I use the development or test data for training?

No. Although the test labels are not accessible, the development and blind test sets must not be used for training or model tuning.

More questions : contact **challenge2026-deepphenotree@inrae.fr**

pages.title: Prizes
pages.content:
# Prizes
One full registration at ICCV 2027 conference, sponsored by **Université d'Angers**:

* **Overall Winner** - Highest mAP@0.5:0.95 on the blind test set.
* **Best Academic Submission** - Highest ranking among submissions from universities or research institutes.

To be eligible, participants must submit **reproducible prediction code and trained models** (Docker image or GitHub repository) by the code-submission deadline (see Timeline).

Submitting a short system **paper (≤ 4 pages) is optional**. 

Participation in the ECCV CVPPA workshop requires full submission of a paper through submission system before 10th July.

description:
The DeepPhenoTree - Apple Edition dataset (1 438 RGB images of apple trees collected in Switzerland, Belgium, Spain and Italy in 2025) contains 22 500 manually annotated bounding boxes for the three phenological stages flowering, fruitlet and fruit.

Develop and benchmark object‑detection models that automatically locate the three phenological stages in the images. Submissions are evaluated with mean Average Precision (mAP@0.5), the standard metric for detection challenges.

The results of the challenge will be presented during the CVPPA workshop (8‑9 September) of the ECCV conference (8‑13 September 2026) in Malmö, Sweden.
reward: One full registration at ICCV 2027 conference
